title: Calling SV class instance results in "Malformed statement"
body:
Hello,
  I'm trying to write a singleton class that has a static function for creating the singleton. 

**function inside the class.....**
```
    static function GlobalSimControl create();
        if (gsc_instance == null) begin
            gsc_instance = new();
            $display("PDS_NOTE: Creating GlobalSimControl singleton");
        end
        else begin
            $display("PDS_NOTE: GlobalSimControl singleton already exists");
        end
        return gsc_instance;
    endfunction
```

However the calling of the static function in my test bench always fails compilation with syntax error and malformed statement.

**In the test bench**
```
   // Setup test bench control
    g_sim_pkg::GlobalSimControl gsc;

    // Initialize the test bench
    initial begin
        gsc=GlobalSimControl::create();   <- this is where the compilation fails (malformed statement)
    end
```

I'm not sure why this is occurring. The code seems to be correct and is able to compile the declaration statement. Does iverilog support static function calls?

Thanks!

Mark
